transcript.text: Hello. I'm done with my kayak. So this is the tour of the ultimate kayak, the UK one. I'd love to hear suggestions on how I can make it better. I think I did pretty good though.

So what I was shooting for was a boat that is fast, is light, is very buoyant, and is very comfortable to be in for a long time. And it's very convenient. I can get all of my stuff without even pulling my skirt.
So, let me show you my boat. There she is. So I'll give a tour of the hull boat. Here's the front. Here's my backpack. Got her clipped in.
Um, right now, all I have in the backpack is, uh, the water pump. So, it just flips in like that. And I can take it out. And this strap, I can put it on top of it.
Pull the backpack up. And then, I have my boxes. Totally waterproof. I completely submerged all of them last night and they do not leave a single bubble. They're all transparent except for that one.

So, I have, you can see. Yes, I have one, two in the front, two in the back. And these are my clothes. And these are my, um, these are my mugs. This is all that we're going to have of, of, uh, silverware for these things. Um, it's actually connected to the stove. I'll show you the stove. So, it's pretty cool.

Here's my clothing. Um, I'm not really going to bring many, many clothes. Like, I'll show you what I'll have for clothing. You've got to have the socks. I don't know, two or three pairs of socks. Got my rain jacket. Got my t-shirt. Got my fleece jacket. Got my silk, silk shirt.

My, uh, sporty t-shirt. My hat. And my jacket. What else? My leggings. And, uh, another pair of socks. So, there's my clothes. Ooh. And also, I'll have a button-up shirt. A normal pair of shorts. And then, my running shorts.

So, let's see here. In here, look at this. There's all this compartment. I will have my first aid kit. My three straps for my hammock, which I shall demonstrate. My sneakers. My seat, which I shall demonstrate.
And, and my dry bag will be my sleeping bag. That's what's going to be up there. Now, up here, as you can see, is connected my fishing pole.
I have the fishing pole and the extra paddle on this side with the same strap. I think that's going to work fine.
I'll just unclip it. I've unclipped and clipped just one of those in the water, and that's pretty simple.
So, a pretty nice fishing pole. Avenger. And it floats and everything. Here's my extra paddle.
And let's see here. From this side, which you can see from there, I have attached. Here, this is how I would take this one thing off when I'm in the boat.
I'll do it like that. I want the paddle. Here's my extra paddle. So, I'll tell why it's, uh, two different colors in just a second.
Here's my sail.
Alrighty. Watch this. I took this sail out the other day. I went out on the lake. Uh, Waltree Long Lake.
And, uh, it's about two miles down. And I went around the bend, and I put up the sail, and in what seemed like a half hour, I was already on the other side of the lake.
I'd gone two miles. It was really cool. And, uh, I was steering myself with the pedals in the back, and it was like driving a car.
It was really fast. It took, like, a couple hours to paddle back, though.
So, here's my sail. The other day, when I went out, I didn't have these straps, and, uh, the sail would flatten out completely.
So, I'm curious to see how much faster I'll go, now that I have these straps. This is how the straps work.
I put them right here. The handle. Works good enough.
And if I want to unstrap it, I think I'm going to break the sail with too strong wind or something, if I can unstrap it quickly.
So, here's what it looks like from a distance. Here's what it looks like from the back.
Pretty big sail. Pretty cool.
So, there's the, there's the sail.
And here's how I have it connected to the sail.
So, it's adjustable.
Okay.
So, let's see.
Okay.
